product-intent.summary: Leetgrind is a local-first web application for programming interview preparation, AI-assisted learning, adaptive mentoring, coding practice, mock interviews, spaced repetition, and knowledge graph based progress tracking.
product-intent.primary-user: A single local user preparing for programming interviews and improving technical skills.
product-intent.strategic-goal: Become a high-quality personal AI mentor for interview preparation and long-term skill growth.

in-scope.item: AI provider abstraction with OpenRouter, OpenAI API key, Codex subscription auth, and future local providers.
RAG over local learning material, user history, resume, attempts, and evaluations.
Adaptive onboarding from user skills, goals, and resume input.
Progress dashboard with strengths, weaknesses, and goal-specific readiness.
Knowledge assessments with AI evaluation and evidence extraction.
Coding practice with editor, deterministic tests, and AI review.
Mock interviews by difficulty and goal.

out-of-scope-for-mvp.item: Marketplace for shared courses or public user profiles.
Running arbitrary native code without sandboxing, timeouts, and resource limits.
Making Codex subscription auth the only AI path.

persona.name: Local learner
persona.description: Uses Leetgrind alone on a personal machine to prepare for technical interviews.

needs.need: Study lessons that match current goals.

functional-requirements.requirement: The user can define initial skills, goals, target roles, and optionally provide resume-like text.
The system tracks skill-level progress, strengths, weaknesses, critical gaps, and goal readiness.
The system can assess user knowledge on a topic and store structured evaluation evidence.
The system can generate or recommend learning items based on goals, skills, and evidence.
The system provides coding tasks, a web editor, deterministic test execution, and AI review.
The system schedules reviews using spaced repetition inspired by SM-2.
The system can run mock interviews by goal and difficulty and produce a readiness report.
The system visualizes skills, prerequisites, related topics, strengths, weaknesses, and next steps.
AI providers are selected behind a provider abstraction and can be changed without changing product flows.
RAG uses local content and memory sources with traceable retrieved context.
The user can inspect prior attempts, mistakes, successes, and progress history.
The user can manage multiple goals without duplicating already-mastered general programming knowledge.

non-functional-requirements.requirement: The app must be local-first by default.
The app must keep AI provider credentials behind provider-specific adapters.
The app must support type-safe contracts across local API and frontend.
The app must preserve domain logic independence from UI, database, and provider SDKs.
The app must produce explainable recommendations based on stored evidence.
The app should be usable on mobile viewports.
Long-running AI workflows should be resumable or at least traceable through agent run records.
Coding execution must have deterministic checks before qualitative AI evaluation.

risks.risk: Codex subscription auth may not support every AI workflow, especially embeddings, background jobs, or structured outputs.
Unsafe user code execution can compromise the local machine if sandboxing is weak.
Storing user memory as unstructured chat history would make recommendations difficult to explain.
Multi-agent implementation without contracts can create drift between domain, persistence, RAG, and UI.
